title: ‫مدونة الحصريات‬‎ Live Stream
Intelligence vs. Wisdom in Islam (And What Iblis Teaches Us) | Snapshots with Imam Tom Facchine
description:
It’s easy to assume that more knowledge automatically makes someone closer to God, but intelligence and wisdom were never the same thing. Some of the most knowledgeable people in Islamic studies aren't practicing Muslims at all: they’re fluent in the language, expert in the texts, and still missing the point entirely. That's exactly what happens when knowledge gets separated from action.

In this Snapshot, Imam Tom Facchine unpacks why intelligence and wisdom are not the same, and why the Qur'an warns that knowledge without action can become a source of harm rather than benefit.

This episode covers:
 — Why knowledge without action breeds hypocrisy
 — The difference between mastering a text and living by it
 — What Iblis's story teaches us about intelligence versus guidance
 — Why the Companions learned the Qur'an differently than we do today

#intelligence #wisdom #islam #knowledge

title: Ask Allah To Change YOU Instead | LATEST Khutbah by Dr. Omar Suleiman
description:
The next time you raise your hands, don’t stop asking Allah to change your circumstances. But before you ask Him to change your world, ask Him to change your heart. Sometimes the greatest answer to a du’a is not that Allah changes what surrounds you, but that He changes who you become within it. 

Memorize this du’a that the Prophet ﷺ would make.

“O Allah grant me Your love and the love of those whose love will benefit me with You. O Allah, whatever you have provided me of that which I love, then make it strength for me for that which You love. O Allah, and what you have kept from me of that which I love, then make it for me a period of rest in that which You love.” 

اللَّهُمَّ ارْزُقْنِي حُبَّكَ وَحُبَّ مَنْ يَنْفَعُنِي حُبُّهُ عِنْدَكَ اللَّهُمَّ مَا رَزَقْتَنِي مِمَّا أُحِبُّ فَاجْعَلْهُ قُوَّةً لِي فِيمَا تُحِبُّ 
اللَّهُمَّ وَمَا زَوَيْتَ عَنِّي مِمَّا أُحِبُّ فَاجْعَلْهُ لِي فَرَاغًا فِيمَا تُحِبُّ 

Allahumma’rzuqni hubbak wa hubba yanfa‘uni hubbuhu indak. Allahumma ma razaqtani mimma uhibbu faj‘alhu quwwatan li fi ma tuhibbu. Allahumma wa ma zawayta anni mimma uhibbu faj‘alhu li faraghan fi ma tuhibbu.  

[Source: Tirmidhi #3491, hasan]

title: The Colonial Playbook: How Muslim Civilizations Were Weakened
description:
Anchor Series #9 | 01/20/26 – The Impact of Colonization on the Muslim World

#YasirQadhi #Colonialism #Civilization 

In the opening lecture of a new Anchor Module, Shaykh Yasir Qadhi examines how colonialism transformed the Muslim world politically, economically, intellectually, and religiously. Tracing the rise of European dominance, he outlines the five major phases of colonialism and explores how Muslim lands were weakened through military conquest, economic exploitation, the dismantling of Islamic institutions, and the creation of modern nation-states.

The lecture also examines the lasting effects of colonialism on Muslim thought and identity, explaining how the disruption of traditional systems contributed to the emergence of secular nationalism, socialism, modernism, progressivism, and fundamentalism. Shaykh Yasir concludes by highlighting Islam’s dynamic relationship with culture and setting the stage for further discussion in the series.

description:

title: Why Did the Angels Prostrate to Adam and Not Eve? | Q&A with Nouman Ali Khan
description:
How could Iblis stand in the presence of Allah and still refuse His command? In this Q&A, Ustadh Nouman Ali Khan answers three questions. The first: why were the angels commanded to prostrate to Adam (AS) and not to Eve as well? Ustadh Nouman explains that Adam (AS) is the first human being, not merely the first man, and that all of his children, including Eve (Hawwa), were honored through that moment. The second: did Iblis lose his awe of Allah? The answer is a psychological diagnosis: his entire self-worth was built on comparison, and the moment he was no longer number one, his desire to protect his image became greater than his recognition of Allah. The third: do we need to master Arabic to understand the Quran? Everyone is on a journey with the Word of Allah, and that is exactly what He wants.

title: What Backbiting Is and Why Muslims Still Do It | Surah Al-Hujurat | Nouman Ali Khan
description:
In this clip from our Deeper Look of Surah Al-Hujurat, Ustadh Nouman Ali Khan unpacks one of the most overlooked dimensions of Ayah 49:12, the verse in which Allah asks whether any of you would love to eat the flesh of your dead brother and you would despise that. Most people hear this verse and think about the person doing the backbiting. Ustadh Nouman shows why that reading is incomplete. 

The grammatical choice Allah makes, addressing the prohibition in the third person rather than directly, is deliberate: it covers not only the one speaking but every person sitting in the room who heard it and said nothing. Silence, in this Ayah, is a violation. 

He then walks through the real reasons people backbite or permit it around them: unaddressed anger, the fear of social awkwardness, a need to deflect attention from their own flaws and jealousy toward others who appear happy. The image Allah chose, eating your own brother's dead flesh, is so viscerally repulsive precisely because our tongues produce harm we have grown numb to. The ugliness of the image is the point. Watch the full Deeper Look episode and the complete series at https://byna.tv/21.

title: What to Do When Someone Never Forgives You
description:
Not everyone who owes you an apology will offer one, and not everyone you apologize to will forgive you. In this reflection, Ustadh Nouman Ali Khan addresses one of the hardest truths about forgiveness: it is not up to you, and it never was. Some people will call you an angel, others will call you a devil for the exact same mistake, and neither verdict changes who you actually are before Allah. Ustadh Nouman Ali Khan draws a clear line between people's judgment and Allah's judgment, one shaped by anger and limited patience, the other rooted in perfect wisdom, justice and mercy. He explains what it means to seek forgiveness sincerely, do your part, then release the outcome, trusting that Allah compensates what people withhold. Let this be the moment you hand that weight over to the only judgment that truly counts.

title: What’s the point of Jesus’ Miracle Birth? | Q&A with Nouman Ali Khan
description:
Why would Allah design the miraculous birth of Isa (AS) in a way that invited people's worst assumptions? In this Q&A, Ustadh Nouman Ali Khan responds to a questioner reflecting on Surah Maryam (19:20), when Maryam (AS) asks how she could have a child when no man has touched her. Ustadh Nouman explains that the test was never really about her. It was a test for the people around her: a believing community that was quick to demonize, chastise and humiliate one of their own without knowing the facts. Allah flipped their assumptions, showing that honor comes from Him alone, and that Maryam (AS) and her son would become two of the most honored human beings in existence. Remarkably, Allah told her not to defend herself, because the more they spoke, the more they exposed themselves. A powerful lesson for anyone who has ever been judged unfairly.

title: Why Reading Into What People Say Poisons Your Heart | Surah Al-Hujurat | Nouman Ali Khan
description:
In this clip from our Deeper Look of Surah Al-Hujurat, Ustadh Nouman Ali Khan unpacks a tension that quietly corrodes Muslim communities: the habit of making bad assumptions about people who have given you no reason to doubt them. Drawing from the Ayah يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اجْتَنِبُوا كَثِيرًا مِّنَ الظَّنِّ إِنَّ بَعْضَ الظَّنِّ إِثْمٌ وَلَا تَجَسَّسُوا (49:12), Ustadh Nouman walks through the scholarly position that suspicion toward someone whose outward conduct is good is simply not permitted and that suspicion left unchecked leads naturally to spying. 

He brings a hadith to life in which the Prophet ﷺ stood before the Ka’bah and swore that the dignity, blood and wealth of a Muslim are more sacred to Allah than the Ka’bah itself, meaning that attacking a believer's honor through assumption is graver than desecrating that sacred house. The clip closes with a practical warning: obsessing over what people really meant when they spoke to you is not cleverness, it is a failure of trust in Allah. Watch the full Deeper Look series on Bayyinah TV at https://byna.tv/21.

title: "At Least I Said It to Your Face"
description:
We think honesty is a virtue. And it is. But calling someone out on their flaws, to their face, isn't honesty. It's humiliation.
 
Ayesha (RA) once made a small gesture about another person's appearance. Just a gesture. No words.
The Prophet ﷺ  said: "You have contaminated your words with it. If that word was thrown into the ocean, the entire ocean would be contaminated."
 
One small gesture. One mocking look. And it poisons everything.
 
Watch your words. Watch your gestures. Watch your eyes.

title: Why Some Sahabah Finished the Quran in a Week and Others Didn't
description:
Not every companion of the Prophet ﷺ read the Quran the same way. Some completed it in a week. Some took ten days. Others read a little every single day. So which one is the standard?
In this reflection, Ustadh Nouman Ali Khan explains why comparing your Quran habits to the most remarkable stories from history sets an unfair bar. The cases that get recorded in the books are often the exceptions, not the norm, and history has to be read in balance, without hyperbole. He draws this back to the closing verses of Surah Al-Muzzammil, where Allah acknowledges that not everyone can do the same amount, so recite what is easy for you of the Quran (73:20).
If your Ramadan Quran goal feels smaller than someone else's, this is a reminder that consistency matters more than comparison.

title: Selective Outrage: Standing Up Against Injustice | Q&A with Nouman Ali Khan
description:
It's easy to speak up about injustice happening a thousand miles away. It's much harder when it's happening in your own family. In this Q&A, Ustadh Nouman Ali Khan responds to a questioner struggling to understand how to live by the command in Surah Al-Ma'idah (5:8): stand up for Allah as witnesses to justice, and never let hatred keep you from being fair. Ustadh Nouman explains that justice is not only about geopolitics and activism. The same Allah who commanded us to pray and fast commanded us to speak up when we see wrong, whether it's financial injustice, family injustice or wrongdoing among our own friends. Staying silent to avoid drama while posting about causes far away is a double standard the Quran does not allow. The goal is a real commitment to the actual word of Allah and what it does in our lives.

title: Your Sarcastic Compliment Is Still an Insult | Surah Al-Hujurat | Nouman Ali Khan
description:
In this clip from Surah Al-Hujurat Deeper Look Series Ustadh Nouman Ali Khan walks through one of the most overlooked forms of harm in Muslim communities: the words we say to each other's faces, the assumptions we hold in our hearts and the nicknames we throw around as jokes.

He breaks down the Arabic distinction between a derogatory nickname (namas) and a harmless title (laqab), and explains why Allah warns us against even sarcastic use of kind words. He also discusses a narration from Aisha (RA) where the Prophet ﷺ told her that a single gesture of mockery contaminated her entire speech and could have contaminated an ocean. 

The clip closes with a powerful reminder that bad assumptions toward another believer are not just a social problem: they are backbiting of the heart and one of the rare sins in Islam where the thought itself is held accountable.

title: How Long Will My Oppressors Keep Getting Away With It? | Q&A with Nouman Ali Khan
description:
What do you do when someone with more power, more leverage and more rights keeps hurting you, and there seem to be no consequences for them at all? Ustadh Nouman Ali Khan answers a question many Muslims quietly carry: the feeling that injustice goes unanswered and that nothing will ever change.

He explains that the power an oppressor holds is given by Allah and can be taken back by Him at any moment. تُؤْتِي الْمُلْكَ مَن تَشَاءُ وَتَنزِعُ الْمُلْكَ مِمَّن تَشَاءُ (3:26). Those who think their cruelty has put them ahead are making a horrible calculation and what they hoard and withhold will become a collar around their necks on Judgment Day (3:180).

The relief Allah promises is real, but its timing and form belong to Him, not to us. That is no excuse for cowardice. We are still called to stand up to the tyrant, even when we are small, and leave the rest in Allah's hands.

title: Why People of Effort Never Burn Out
description:
Why do so many of us feel broken when we don't see results, no matter how hard we work? Ustadh Nouman Ali Khan describes a different kind of person: one who isn't crushed by the absence of results because they are sustained by the effort itself.
Using the feeling after a hard workout, he explains how sincere effort carries its own motivation before any final goal is reached. A day of diligent, honest work is a successful day, worth celebrating on its own. This mindset keeps you from living trapped in the future you haven't reached or the past you regret, and roots you firmly in the present.

title: The Taqwa Nobody Talks About | Nouman Ali Khan
description:
We think taqwa is between us and Allah. Prayer, fasting, worship, purely spiritual.

But these ayat flip that understanding completely.

Making peace between believers is taqwa. Fixing your relationships is taqwa. How you treat people around you is how you show your fear and consciousness of Allah.

And there's a warning too, even when your intentions are good, the ends don't justify the means. Don't cross lines in the name of doing good.

Taqwa isn't just what happens on your prayer mat. It shows up in how you deal with people.

title: Brotherhood Has No Madhab | Nouman Ali Khan
description:
Allah described the believers as absolutely humble before one another. Not just respectful, humbled. A bond thicker than blood.

But today? We've replaced that with madhabs, schools of thought, institutes, and sheikhs. We put a hundred qualifications before we'll call someone our brother.

"إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ إِخْوَةٌ", The believers are nothing but brothers.

That's it. No conditions. No labels. Just La ilaha illa Allah and before you stands your Muslim brother.

We had the most powerful bond in the world. And we made it weak.

title: You Can't Have Taqwa and Ignore People | Surah Al-Hujurat | Nouman Ali Khan
description:
In this clip from Surah Al-Hujurat Deeper Look Series, Ustadh Nouman Ali Khan talks about one of the most striking claims in the Quran: that true believers are nothing but blood brothers. A bond defined not by ethnicity, language, school of thought or circle, but by iman alone.

Ustadh Nouman walks through the ayah وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ (49:10) and explains why making peace between believers is not optional goodwill but an act of taqwa itself. He shares a hadith about aiding both the wrongdoer and the wronged, explaining that helping someone who is in the wrong means stopping them from wrongdoing, that is how you truly help them. He also reflects on the price of fairness: a truly fair judgment often leaves everyone unhappy and yet Allah says He loves those who avoid unfairness.

The clip closes with a direct, honest challenge about how Muslims today filter brotherhood through nationality, madhab, wealth, immigration status and even the way someone wears hijab and why that, according to this surah, is the very definition of jahaliyyah.

title: Why People of Effort Are Never Broken by Failure
description:
There's a difference between not caring about results and not being broken when the results don't come. People of effort care deeply, but their hearts aren't crushed by what they can't control. The effort itself is what sustains them.
In this clip, Ustadh Nouman Ali Khan draws a striking parallel to something many of us know well. Anyone who works out understands that finishing the session is a win, even before the body transforms. That feeling, this was worth it, this day was a success, is exactly the mindset the Quran trains in us.
When you celebrate sincere effort right now, you stop living in the regret of the past or the anxiety of the future. You live in the present, anchored by Alhamdulillah and the work in front of you. Even modern conversations about mental health circle back to what Islam taught all along.
Let's become people of effort, and celebrate it.

title: How Can Muslims Help America? l President Hamza Yusuf l Zaytuna College
description:
American Muslims have long struggled to integrate these two seemingly polarized aspects of their identities. At Zaytuna College, they discover that they need not experience identity crises. They deepen their understanding of the ways these two aspects of their identity are compatible. They discover that Islam belongs in the west — having first arrived on American soil during the slave trade — and has an important role to play in America’s realization of its highest ideals. 

In honor of thirty years of Zaytuna College this year, we share this clip from the 1990s, President Hamza Yusuf reflects on the important role Muslims play in helping America realize its highest ideals.  
 
Zaytuna College, the first accredited Muslim liberal arts college in the United States, offers a rigorous liberal arts education grounded in the great works of the Islamic and Euro-American traditions. description:
What happens when your college education nourishes both your mind and your soul?

In this clip, 2026 graduating senior Abdel reflects on his four years at Zaytuna College, America's first accredited Muslim liberal arts college, and what it truly meant to be surrounded by likeminded peers and scholars who pushed him to think deeper, ask harder questions, and grow closer to God in the process.

From studying the great texts of the Western and Islamic traditions to building a community rooted in shared values and sincere pursuit of knowledge, his journey is a testament to what education looks like when it doesn't separate the intellectual from the spiritual.

Is a Zaytuna right for you or someone you know? Explore our programs: https://zaytuna.edu/academics

Zaytuna College is located in Berkeley, California and offers a BA in Liberal Arts and Islamic Studies and an MA in Islamic Texts.

title: Shaykh Hamza Yusuf on AI, Reading, and Human Wisdom | Book Club Selections for Muharram 2026
description:
This Muharram, the First Command Book Club reads two important works that explore the promises and challenges of artificial intelligence: "The AI Mirror: How to Reclaim Our Humanity in an Age of Machine Thinking" by Shannon Vallor, and "Magnifica Humanitas," an encyclical letter by Pope Leo XIV. 

Encyclical letters address matters of significance to the wider human community. Throughout history, pontiffs wrote encyclicals on subjects ranging from labor and social justice to environmental stewardship. As communities across the world grapple with the implications of artificial intelligence, this recent encyclical offers an opportunity to consider how one major religious tradition responds to these questions. Muslims, too, must think carefully about the ethical and spiritual challenges posed by emerging technologies and contribute meaningful guidance to the conversation. 

At Zaytuna College, we strive to preserve and cultivate natural intelligence. Reading remains one of the most important means by which we do so—and God willing, by which we rise. 

Join us as we embark on the sixth year of the First Command Book Club. We hope to see you there. 

Learn More about the First Command Book Club and Join: https://bookclub.zaytuna.edu/

title: Formal Logic II: Judgement and Reasoning | Zaytuna College Online Course
description:
This online course — the second taught by Dr. Phillbert Cheng in a two–part series offered by Zaytuna College — continues the study of formal logic by exploring judgement, propositions, and reasoning. 

Students in this course will learn how thoughts are structured, claims are tested, and arguments are formed.  This course offers a sample of Zaytuna College’s singular liberal arts curriculum comprising the great works of the Islamic and Euro-American traditions. 

The purpose of a classic liberal arts education is to provide the foundational tools for lifelong learning and to train students to think clearly, communicate persuasively, and contribute meaningfully to the greater good.  For many, logic was considered the jewel of the trivium — grammar, logic, and rhetoric — as it cultivated the essence of what it is to be human: the ability to reason. 

All of Zaytuna's online courses are available to stream on demand and available to take at your own pace.

title: The Unshakable Believer: Standing Firm When the World Judges
description:
Holding Onto Your Faith in a Judgmental World (The Great Reversal) 📌 Note to viewers: While we are blessed to live alongside an open-minded and respectful majority in the West, this lecture addresses the specific moments when that tolerance breaks down—not only among hostile non-Muslims but equally among Muslim families and communities themselves. The Quran does not analyze hostility to breed negativity; it does so to validate the quiet struggles of believers and to provide an unshakeable blueprint for psychological resilience. 
This video explores the divine psychology of social pressure as mapped out in the Quran. In this deep dive into Surat Al-Mutaffifin, we break down the exact psychological warfare faced by practicing Muslims navigating secular spaces today. From social media mockery to workplace or university isolation, discover how Allah validates the pain of the lonely believer, exposes the toxic insecurity of the mocker, and perfectly reverses the cosmic scales so that final triumph belongs to those who refuse to bow to social pressure. 
If you found value in this reminder, please Like, Subscribe, and Share this video to support the channel and help spread the message of unshakable faith

title: Episode 15: Contentment (Al-Riḍā, Part 2) | Change of Heart Series
description:
Episode 16: https://youtu.be/UTkh9vU5LAs

Why do some people stay at peace while their life falls apart, whilst others with everything still feel empty? In this episode of the Change of Heart series, we Ridā, contentment with whatever Allah decrees.
Through the stories of Hajar in the desert, the Prophet's own losses, and Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas choosing blindness over complaint, he shows what real contentment looks like under pressure, not passive resignation, but a settled heart.
We outline what it brings: forgiveness, the sweetness of faith, dignity, the good life and ultimately Allah's pleasure, then closes with practical steps, saying alhamdulillah through hardship, dropping comparison, remembering death, dua, and truly knowing Allah.

A grounding watch for anyone exhausted by chasing more.

Episode 1 of the series: https://youtu.be/vLb4YF-0F5M

title: Episode 14: Contentment (Al-Riḍā, Part 1) | Change of Heart Series
description:
The modern machine feeds on discontent, as it is precisely what keeps people consuming, scrolling, chasing, and never arriving. Here, Islam calls the believer to something far higher: al-riḍā, serene contentment with Allah.

We explore: How does riḍā differ from ṣabr (patience), tawakkul (reliance), and shukr (gratitude)? And what are the signs that a heart is truly content with Allah?

title: Why Hasan (RA) Chose Peace Instead of War | Sh Ammar Alshukry
description:
Why Hasan (RA) Chose Peace Instead of War

Many people think choosing peace means giving up.
But Hasan ibn Ali (RA), the grandson of the Prophet ﷺ, showed that true strength is knowing when peace serves the Ummah better than conflict.

Sometimes the strongest leader isn't the one who fights.
It's the one who prevents bloodshed.

What do you think was the greatest lesson from Hasan's (RA) decision?

Watch the full lecture on our YouTube channel

#Islam #Muslim #HasanIbnAli #Leadership #Reminder #AlMaghrib

title: How Shaytan Actually Attacks You | Sh. Saad Tasleem | Al Maghrib Podcast
description:
What if you could study Shaytan the way a therapist studies a patient, by analyzing his own words in the Quran? 

Sh. Saad Tasleem joins Sh. Ammar AlShukry to talk about his new book Shaytan, a comprehensive study built from more than ten years of teaching.

They get into the psychology of Shaytan, why we were given so few physical descriptions of him, how his attacks are tailored to your specific fears and desires, and the step by step method he uses to move a person from a small slip to something far worse. Along the way there are stories worth hearing, including a thobe shop in Medina that revealed how Shaytan really operates, and honest talk about fame, ego, and why every one of us needs people who will tell us the truth.

Sh. Saad teaches Deception with AlMaghrib and his new book 'Shaytan' can be found at AlMaghrib events in your city: https://www.almaghrib.org

Chapters

1:04 The Viking vest and when to give fashion advice

title: A Poem About Beauty and the Divine | Sh Ammar Alshukry
description:
Have you ever seen a dog stunned by a sunset, or a bird taken by the sea below it? Sh. Ammar AlShukry shares a poem he wrote about beauty and what our love of it says about us. If we were only animals like the rest, why are our souls so easily captured by beautiful places, beautiful moments, and beautiful people? 

Maybe the One who made beauty also made us, and our love of it points us back to Him.

title: What Happens When Aliens Claim They Created Us | Sh Fahad Tasleem
description:
Sh. Fahad Tasleem lays out why this matters. The deception does not arrive all at once. It moves step by step. First people are asked to accept the idea of extraterrestrials, which is theologically possible for us. Then comes the disclosure that says these beings are here, and by the way, they created you. At that point you are forced to backtrack and scramble for a response. His point is to think ahead to the moment aliens are claimed to be our creators, so we are ready if it ever happens. This is not something every Muslim needs to dive into, but leaders of the community should at least be familiar with it.

title: Remembering Srebrenica
description:
This weekend marks the 31st anniversary of one of the darkest events for the Ummah and humanity in recent history: The massacre of Srebrenica.

During the Bosnian war in the 90s, as part of a genocidal campaign against the Bosnian Muslims, Serbian forces gathered men and boys from the town of Srebrenica, systematically murdering them, whilst brutalising the women and girls of the town.

It’s reported that over 8000 men and boys were brutally killed in the ethnic cleansing, with many bodies still being uncovered to this day, three decades later, with an annual collective janazah for the newly identified victims.

It was a crime reflecting the ugly and enduring legacy of Islamophobia, one that we still see around us today.

Our own Shaykh Yaser Birjas, who spent time doing aid work in the aftermath of the war, took a group of our students to visit the memorial site at Srebrenica, sharing some important reflections on remembering the martyrs of Bosnia.

May Allah have mercy on the Shuhada of yesterday and today, and protect our brothers and sisters facing oppression across the globe.

title: Schoolchildren Who All Drew the Same Alien | Sh Fahad Tasleem
description:
Sh. Fahad Tasleem points to John Mack, a well known Harvard psychiatrist who spent years studying alien abduction accounts as a psychological and psychiatric phenomenon. He was ridiculed for it, but the cases are well documented, and people describe the same thing across different parts of the world. The classic image of the gray, with the big eyes and the small body, keeps showing up. In one documented case in Zimbabwe, schoolchildren said they watched a craft land and interacted with these beings. Asked to draw what they saw, they nearly all drew the same figure. Interviewed years later as adults, they still stand by their story. They were children at the time, and they had no reason to lie.

Watch the full episode on the AlMaghrib Podcast!
